summary: Typosquat of big.js silently requires undeclared sibling package on import
details: Package ships a verbatim copy of big.js v7.0.1 (description, keywords, and repository URL all point at MikeMcl/big.js) but inserts a try/catch-suppressed `require("parket-slot")` followed by `doc.from_str()` at module top level in both big.js and big.mjs. The 'parket-slot' module is not declared in package.json's dependencies — only 'new-solt: 0.0.7' is — so the call attempts to resolve and execute attacker-controlled code from a sibling package that the consumer never asked for, with all errors silently swallowed to hide failures. The legitimate big.js library has zero runtime dependencies; this package adds one ('new-solt') matching the same author's naming scheme as the hidden require target, staging a coordinated multi-package drop where the carrier looks benign while the malicious payload lives in separate packages the same actor controls. Any code that imports new-ecro triggers this hidden execution path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
